summary.intent: Fix GitHub issue #656 where agents.presets tools.deny silently has no effect on the main agent session, misleading users who configure deny lists under presets expecting main-session security enforcement.
summary.outcome: Added a validation warning, improved doc comments, and updated the config template to clarify sub-agent-only scope of preset tool policies. All 192 tests pass, fmt and clippy clean, committed and ready for PR.

repo: agents.presets tool policies apply ONLY to sub-agents spawned via spawn_agent; main session tool filtering requires [tools.policy]
Validation diagnostics use a category string taxonomy in validate.rs; 'security' was chosen as the category for the new silent-misconfig warning
Commit shape follows conventional commits: fix(scope): message; git-cliff handles CHANGELOG generation automatically
Quality gates: cargo +nightly-2025-11-30 fmt --all, just lint, cargo test -p moltis-config
The config template (template.rs) round-trips through load_config in loader.rs tests, so template edits are implicitly covered by the full test suite

code.finding: Existing default_preset validation check — new silent-misconfig warning was added adjacent to this block
AgentsConfig struct and default_preset field — doc comments were too terse and did not warn about sub-agent-only scope
AgentPreset struct — tools field access pattern: !preset.tools.allow.is_empty() || !preset.tools.deny.is_empty()
SPAWN PRESETS section header — renamed to SUB-AGENT SPAWN PRESETS with a scope warning note added
[tools.policy] section — added reciprocal comment clarifying this is main-session only

workflow: Plan was shared with user for approval before coding; open questions on warning granularity and trigger conditions were resolved upfront
Tests were written alongside the feature (3 cases: trigger, no-trigger variants, edge cases) before running quality gates
fmt was applied (not just checked) when it found issues, then verified clean before proceeding to clippy

friction: Needed multiple reads of schema.rs to locate ToolPolicyConfig and confirm the allow/deny field access pattern before writing the validation logic
Template test coverage was not obvious — required tracing through loader.rs to confirm template edits were already covered by the existing 192-test suite
open_items: Breaking rename of default_preset to default_subagent_preset was intentionally deferred — not worth the churn per reporter and user agreement

No behavioral bridge added between preset tool policies and the main session (effective_tool_policy in crates/chat/src/lib.rs) — would require separate design discussion on precedence rules and interaction with profile
PR was committed and pushed but transcript ends before PR creation is confirmed
